free_text: Production cross sections of radioisotopes from
3He-particle induced nuclear reactions on natural
titanium
Targets were natural Ti: 30 and 20 microns
The activities of the produced radionuclides were
measured non-destructively without chemical separation.
The energy degradation as a function of depth in the
stack was calculated from the input energy and target
thicknesses.
Decay data were taken from NuDat2.6 database.
The uncertainty of the primary beam energy
was estimated as (+-0.2 MeV) which increased due to
straggling effect continuously in each foil through the
stack.
The total uncertainties were determined by
summing the following uncertainties in quadrature.
number of bombarding particles(3-5%)
beam intensity loss(0-3%)
surface density of target atoms(1-2%)
detector efficiency fitting(<2%)
calibration sources activity(3%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-5%)
The experimental results were compared to the
result calculated by TALYS code and taken from TENDL
database.
